channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: China Snaps Up Russian Oil as Mideast Risks Mount
item.description: Chinese buyers are snapping up Russia's flagship ESPO crude weeks earlier than usual, as heightened risks to Middle East oil flows prompt refiners to secure supplies before they're needed.

item.title: TotalEnergies reports $5.4bn net income in Q2 2026
item.description: TotalEnergies reported a net income of $5.4bn in the second quarter of 2026 (Q2 2026), a 100% increase from $2.7bn in the same period of the previous year.

item.title: U.S. Prioritizes Small Nuclear Reactors Across Southeast Asia
item.description: The U.S. has identified cooperation on small modular nuclear reactors (SMRs) as a key priority in its engagement with Southeast Asia as regional electricity demand continues to climb. Speaking after Secretary of State…

item.title: Global LNG Giants Target Cambodia as New Energy Market
item.description: Some of the world’s biggest heavyweights in liquefied natural gas exports are interested in adding Cambodia to their client list as the Southeast Asian nation build sits first LNG-fueled power station. The United…

item.title: Trump Threatens Iran After Houthi Tanker Attacks
item.description: President Trump has threatened to “punish” Iran for the Yemeni Houthis’ recent attacks on tankers in the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, saying, “If they do this again, the U.S. will hold Iran responsible, in that the Houthis are…

item.title: Oil Tops $100 as Supply Crisis Deepens
item.description: Crude oil prices were on course for a sizable weekly gain today, as the Strait of Hormuz remains almost entirely paralysed and the Yemeni Houthis target tankers in the Bab el-Mandeb Strait in the Red Sea. To make…

item.title: Matador Buys Permian Assets for $1.3B
item.description: Matador Resources Co. agreed to buy Permian Basin assets from EnCap Investments LP for about $1.3 billion in cash.

item.title: Oil Surges Above $100
item.description: Oil climbed above $100 a barrel after Houthi attacks on Saudi tankers heightened fears of broader supply disruptions.

item.title: Lithium Prices Sink To Five-Month Lows Amid Oversupply Jitters
item.description: Lithium prices have sunk to a five-month low as the return of previously idled mine capacity triggered fears of a massive market oversupply. Lithium prices on the Guangzhou Futures Exchange (GFEX), the most active…

item.title: Trump Administration Eyes Offshore Nuclear to Power AI Boom
item.description: The Trump administration is studying the deployment of offshore nuclear power generation, with the Marine Minerals Administration and the Nuclear Regulatory Commission agreeing this week to coordinate on any future…

item.title: OPEC+ Set to Raise Output Again—Even as Members Can't Pump It
item.description: OPEC+ is expected to approve another production target increase when eight key producers meet on August 2, extending a months-long campaign to unwind voluntary supply cuts that have existed largely on paper since the…

item.title: The AI Paradox Reshaping Clean Energy Research
item.description: Artificial intelligence is a double-edged sword for the energy sector. The rapid buildout of data centers to support widespread integration of large language models in virtually every economic sector imaginable, from…

item.title: Magnolia to Buy WildFire in $4B Deal
item.description: 'The transaction supports and reinforces Magnolia's business model' Magnolia said.

item.title: Insurers Flock to Oil Projects Outside the Middle East
item.description: Global insurers had just shaken off the ESG push from earlier this decade when the Middle East conflict upended oil and gas upstream project coverage. The world’s lowest-cost oil and gas producing region became a war…

item.title: Eni Starts Producing Gas Power at Kazakhstan Hybrid Project
item.description: 'This milestone marks another major step in the development of Kazakhstan's first large-scale hybrid power plant, integrating solar, gas and future wind generation to ensure decarbonized and reliable electricity supply…

item.title: QatarEnergy Prepares to Extend LNG Force Majeure
item.description: QatarEnergy is preparing to further extend force majeure on liquefied natural gas shipments through mid-October, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Commodities Beat Tech This Decade, and Wall Street Still Won't Buy In
item.description: The best performing asset class of this decade is also the most under owned. Since October 2020, when we first called for a decades-long super-cycle in commodities, the broad commodity indices like the S&P GSCI are up…

item.title: Equinor Makes New Oil Discovery Near Johan Castberg
item.description: Equinor and its partners made a new oil discovery that they are now considering developing via existing infrastructure in the Johan Castberg field on Norway's side of the Barents Sea.

item.title: ABL secures MWS contract for PNG’s first offshore FSO facility
item.description: ABL Group has been appointed by Oil Search (PNG) to provide marine warranty survey services (MWS) for PNG first offshore FSO facility.

item.title: Ukraine’s Drone War Is Choking Kazakhstan’s Oil Exports
item.description: Kazakhstan should, in theory, be one of the major beneficiaries of Russia’s growing isolation from global energy markets. Its crude is unsanctioned and European refiners need alternatives to Russian barrels. Yet instead…
